text: We perform arterial and venous femoral cannulation, needle placement of cardioplasia in aortian root and then aortian clamping.
During the inspection we can observe the prolapse predominantly of Pichu.
We visualize mitral insufficiency in segment Pichu related to the prolapse of such segments and core break.
The stitches are placed on the mitral ring before starting the resection.
After the placement of stitches for the mitral ring, we proceed with the triangular resection in segment P2.
We start a continuous suture from the free edge of the posterior butt, rejected to arrive at the base of the same.
Once we arrive with the suture at the vertices of the triangular resection, we return to the free edge to finish.
After the triangular resection we can see that the mitral insufficiency persists therefore we decide to place a neocord.
We continue with the attachment of the cortex cord.
We conclude the metro repair with the placing of an incomplete ring number 32.
The methylene blue tests show good coaptation of the ball without material insufficiency.
The transesophageal echocardiogram shows us absence of mitral insufficiency.
